items.title: OpenAI's strongest model is gated
items.oneliner: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Soul is limited by government review, signaling frontier model access is now a policy bottleneck.

items.title: AI is entering RF chip design
items.oneliner: Princeton shows AI can tackle RF chip design, pushing a scarce expert craft toward faster, more automatable hardware iteration.

items.title: Hybrid models get token-level scrutiny
items.oneliner: This paper argues token-level evaluation exposes where hybrid architectures beat pure Transformers, which matters for real deployment tradeoffs.

items.title: AI-assisted zero-days are being dumped
items.oneliner: An anonymous GitHub account is publishing undisclosed 0-days and says AI already accelerates its fuzzing workflow.

items.title: The old UEFI trust root expired
items.oneliner: Microsoft's 2011 UEFI CA expired on June 27, so outdated Linux systems now risk secure boot failures.

items.title: Reddit's spam controls got exposed
items.oneliner: A moderator's peek into Reddit spam logs shows how opaque automated enforcement remains, which should worry platform builders.

items.title: Pomerium keeps replacing the VPN
items.oneliner: Pomerium's identity-aware access proxy keeps pushing zero-trust deeper into app delivery, reducing the case for legacy VPN sprawl.

items.title: OpenZL targets AI data compression
items.oneliner: OpenZL claims 203 MB/s compression and 822 MB/s decompression for specialized datasets, making storage optimization workload-specific again.

items.title: ClickHouse rewrote WAL-G in Rust
items.oneliner: ClickHouse built WAL-RUS to speed PostgreSQL backups, another sign Rust is winning boring but critical infrastructure.

items.title: Postgres now lets you steer plans
items.oneliner: PostgreSQL 19's pg_plan_advice gives engineers a mini-language to pin planner choices when the optimizer gets production wrong.
